title: Project Radar — Svelte Tree Component for Milestone/Phase/Step Status
description: Build the Project Radar frontend component: a self-built Svelte 5 tree that renders the milestone->phase->step hierarchy with status indicators. Reads from the projectState store. Supports expand/collapse, status coloring, and translated labels based on display mode.
action: Create src/lib/client/components/ProjectRadar.svelte with a recursive tree rendering milestone->phase->step hierarchy. Each node shows translated name (from translatedName field or name fallback), status indicator (colored dot: green=complete, blue=running, yellow=pending, red=failed), and expand/collapse toggle. Read from projectState store. Import displayMode store for simple/advanced label rendering. Replace the placeholder Project Status section in +page.svelte with this component.

criteria: Component template contains {#each} iteration over $projectState.milestones, milestone.phases, and phase.steps
Each tree node displays translatedName ?? name as label
Status indicators use colored dots: green for 'complete', blue for 'running', yellow for 'pending', red for 'failed'
Expand/collapse toggle works on milestone and phase nodes (step nodes are leaf)
+page.svelte imports ProjectRadar from $lib/client/components/ProjectRadar.svelte and renders it in sidebar section
Component uses (translatedName ?? name) for all user-visible labels — template contains translatedName
grep -E " (delegate|session|skill) " src/lib/client/components/ProjectRadar.svelte returns 0 matches in user-visible string literals (not import paths or comments)
convergence.verification: npx vite build exits 0 && manual visual check in browser
convergence.definition_of_done: Project Radar tree component displays real-time project status with translated labels, status indicators, and expand/collapse interaction

files.change: Create Svelte 5 component with recursive tree rendering. Props: none (reads from stores). Uses projectState store for data, displayMode store for label mode. Renders milestone->phase->step with expand/collapse and status dots.

implementation: Create src/lib/client/components/ProjectRadar.svelte. Script section: import projectState, displayMode from '$lib/client/stores/index.js'. Define helper function statusColor(status:string): string returning CSS color values: 'complete'->'#a6e3a1', 'running'->'#89b4fa', 'pending'->'#f9e2af', 'failed'->'#f38ba8', default->'#6c7086'. Define helper statusIcon(status:string): string returning dot characters.
Define expand/collapse state: let expandedNodes = $state<Set<string>>(new Set()). Function toggleNode(id:string) toggles id in the Set.

Template section: iterate over $projectState.milestones. Each milestone node: click toggles expand/collapse, shows status dot + translatedName??name + status text. When expanded, iterate milestone.phases. Each phase node: same pattern. When expanded, iterate phase.steps. Step nodes: leaf nodes showing status dot + translatedName??name.
Note: +page.svelte wiring is handled by TASK-004

success_metrics: Component renders in browser without console errors

reference.pattern: Svelte 5 runes ($state, $effect) from +page.svelte. Store subscription pattern from stores/index.ts. Color scheme from +page.svelte CSS.

rationale.chosen_approach: Self-built tree component instead of external library because tree depth is fixed at 3 levels (milestone->phase->step) and node count is small. No virtualization needed. Keeps bundle size minimal.
decision_factors: No need for drag-and-drop or lazy loading in MVP
Existing CSS color scheme should be reused
rationale.tradeoffs: Custom tree requires more code than a library, but avoids dependency and gives full control over styling and behavior.

risks.risk: projectState store empty if server not running
risks.mitigation: Component shows empty state message 'No project loaded' when milestones array is empty (matching existing behavior)
